92: docker-compose backend service (read-only, no-new-privileges, health-checked) & Setup and Usage Guide Group
93: docker-compose frontend service (read-only, depends_on backend healthy)
95: ADR-0001 Repository Layout: monorepo with docs/backend/frontend/packages/tests/scripts/infrastructure/.github; rejected multi-repo (fragments context) and framework-first folders (weakens domain ownership)
96: ADR-0002 Local-First Architecture: core workflows, storage, search, scheduling and default AI path operate locally; cloud is an optional adapter, never mandatory; rejected SaaS-first and offline-cache-with-cloud-authority for conflicting with product principles / subordinating local operation
97: ADR-0003 PKOS (Personal Knowledge Operating System): canonical knowledge subsystem storing normalized entities, typed relationships, provenance, temporal validity; source artifacts remain immutable evidence, derived summaries/embeddings are replaceable projections; rejected vector-DB-as-primary-memory (no authoritative relationships) and notes-only model (no cross-domain reasoning)
98: ADR-0004 AI Runtime: all model calls pass through a single AI Runtime (Model Router, prompt registry, structured-output validation, tool permission checks, evaluation hooks, audit logging); AI outputs are proposals until validated; rejected direct model calls per feature for fragmenting policy/evaluation/observability
99: Model Router: centralized component through which all LLM/model calls must pass (provider routing, local/cloud selection, auditable versions)
100: ADR-0005 Event Bus: versioned immutable domain events, past-tense named, standard envelope, published after commit, idempotent consumers, at-least-once delivery, ordering only within an aggregate stream; Phase 0 uses in-process durable implementation behind an event-bus contract; rejected direct service-to-service calls for synchronous coupling/cascading failure
104: AI Contributions Policy: AI-generated code welcome but must never invent APIs, requirements, technologies or architecture; reviewed like human code
146: Attention UX Ethics and Accessibility Constraints
165: Failure Never Blocks Deterministic Core Flow
167: Approval Responses Require Current Version and Exact Action Digest
176: Mandatory Per-Run Approval for High-Risk Action Classes
178: Stable Action Digest and Idempotency Key
179: Bounded Exponential Backoff Only for Classified Transient Failures
180: Compensation Executes Only Declared, Approved Steps
181: Worker Persists State Before/After Each Side Effect
182: Preview-Only Dogfood With Explicit Exit Review
187: Optional Mutations Route Through Approved Automation Policies
191: Connector Creation Never Returns Token Values
192: Cursor Persists Only After Durable Projection
193: Provider Deletion, Access Loss, Rename and Disconnect Are Distinct States
194: Least-Privilege Tokens With Encrypted Secret Storage
196: Connector Payloads Untrusted, Cannot Issue Runtime Instructions
206: Provider/External-ID Scoped Unique Keys With Full Provenance
214: No Composite Engineer Score, Ranking or Leaderboard
215: Risk Signals Cite Underlying Evidence and State Confidence
216: Metric-Definition Changes Create New Version, Preserve History
220: Never Display Person Rankings or Shame Language
223: APIs Enforce Consent and Field Policy Server-Side
226: Health/Finance Suggestions Never Use Diagnostic or Guaranteed-Return Language
235: Insights Are Derived, Versioned, Deletable; Source Records Authoritative
241: Default Search and Meeting Context Exclude Personal Domains
242: Deletion Removes Authoritative and Derived Content
243: Sensitive Data Never Leaves Device Unless Explicitly Allowed
244: Feedback Cannot Silently Turn Correlation Into Causation
245: Cross-Domain Insight Requires Active Grant Covering Every Source
246: Insights Must Be Evidence-Backed, Proportionate, Non-Manipulative
248: System Does Not Diagnose, Prescribe, or Promise Financial Outcomes
251: Calm, Non-Judgmental Language; No Shame, Addiction Loops, or False Urgency
267: Recipient Access Limited to Required Evidence
271: Background Job Re-check Before Side Effects
272: Deny and Privacy Override Role Grants
273: Checks at Service and Query Boundaries
274: Adversarial Tests (IDOR, Privilege Escalation, Confused Deputy)
288: Exceptions Expire and Require Risk Acceptance
289: Legal Hold Prevents Deletion, Not Access Control
292: External Key Material, Stored References Only
293: Legal Hold Preserves Records Without Read Access
298: No Tenant Content in Global Models Without Consent
299: Tenant-scoped Storage, Cache, Jobs, AI Context
309: Documentation Fitness Functions AFF-DOC-001..010: FR/test/ADR traceability, no drift, owned/versioned/changelogged docs, no circular dependencies
310: GOV-001: The specification is authoritative; code implements the specification, never the reverse
311: GOV-002: Every architectural decision is documented
312: GOV-003: Every implementation traces back to requirements
313: GOV-004: Every behaviour-changing change updates documentation
319: EP-001 Specification Before Code: development follows Understand->Specify->Review->Implement->Test->Deploy; no behavior-changing PR without spec update
320: EP-002 Simplicity Wins: the simplest solution satisfying a requirement SHALL be preferred; complexity compounds
321: EP-003 Delete Before Add: evaluate existing systems before introducing new ones; adding code is easy, removing is difficult
322: EP-004 AI is a Junior Engineer: AI accelerates but does not replace engineering judgment; every AI change needs human review, architectural validation, tests, traceability
323: EP-005 Local First: local execution is default for privacy, ownership, latency, resilience, cost; cloud SHALL NOT be mandatory for core workflows
324: EP-006 Explainability Over Intelligence: an explainable recommendation beats an opaque one; unexplainable recommendations SHOULD NOT be shown
325: EP-007 Human Authority: AI recommends, humans decide; AI SHALL NEVER silently send email, delete data, modify data, execute workflows or approve requests
326: EP-008 Permanent Memory: information should never be intentionally discarded; system prefers preservation over deletion; summaries regenerate, memory persists
327: EP-009 Modular Architecture: subsystems have one responsibility, communicate through contracts; dependency direction UI->Application->Domain->Infrastructure, never reversed
328: EP-010 Evolution Over Perfection: choose solutions that evolve safely rather than predicting every future requirement; stability over perfection
329: DP-001 Attention Is The Primary Resource: UI must prioritize action, decisions, risks, commitments and deprioritize stats/vanity metrics/decorative widgets
330: DP-002 Local First: local execution is default architecture for privacy, speed, ownership, offline capability, lower cost; cloud extends but never owns the product
331: DP-003 Explain Everything: every recommendation should answer why, what evidence, how confident, what happens if ignored
332: DP-004 Memory Is Permanent: ECC continuously builds richer understanding of people, projects, orgs, commitments, meetings, decisions; deleting context is exceptional
333: DP-005 Progressive Disclosure: users see only what is required now (Dashboard->Project->Meeting->Conversation->Original Email); present maximum relevance not maximum information
334: DP-006 Calm Interfaces: avoid blinking, animations, unnecessary colors, repeated alerts, graph-filled dashboards; whitespace, silence and focus are features
335: DP-007 Relationships Over Documents: ECC organizes around Meeting->People->Decisions->Projects->Action Items->Risks rather than files; documents are evidence, relationships are knowledge
336: DP-008 Context Before Content: every piece of information is accompanied by participants, previous meeting, related decisions, risks, documents, action items
337: DP-009 Human In Control: AI recommends, humans decide; all state-changing actions (send email, delete, schedule, delegate, update tasks) require explicit confirmation
338: DP-010 AI Should Feel Invisible: users should experience outcomes, not AI; good AI is quiet infrastructure, not the product
339: DP-011 Executive Dashboard First: every widget must answer what needs attention, what changed, what is blocked, what is at risk, what should happen next
340: DP-012 Reduce Decisions: prefer automatic classification/linking/organization/prioritization over manual folder/category/tag/label choices
341: DP-013 Search Is A Backup: information should arrive when relevant; search exists for recovery, not primary navigation
342: DP-014 Time Is A First-Class Entity: ECC stores timelines not files; everything answers when, what changed, before/after
343: DP-015 Design For Years: every feature must scale to 100k emails, 20k meetings, 5k documents, 50k tasks or be redesigned
344: DP-016 Small Surfaces: interfaces expose only a few primary actions; configuration is not capability
345: DP-017 One Source Of Truth: every concept has exactly one owner - Calendar->Calendar Service, Tasks->Task Engine, Knowledge->Knowledge Graph, Models->Model Router
346: DP-018 Consistency Over Novelty: reuse interaction patterns, layouts, terminology; novelty increases learning cost
347: DP-019 Defaults Matter: every default represents the recommended path; zero-configuration onboarding is the long-term goal
348: DP-020 Every Screen Must Earn Its Place: before adding a screen ask if it could be integrated, contextual, automatic, or eliminated
349: Approved-Later Technologies: Ollama, Neo4j, Qdrant, pgvector, Redis, NATS, Kafka, Temporal, Tauri, Kubernetes, S3-compatible storage - each gated by ADR/benchmark/phase activation
350: Prohibited In Phase 0: floating versions, unpinned images, Neo4j, Qdrant, Redis, Kafka, NATS, Temporal, Kubernetes, cloud-only deps, JWT browser sessions, no-auth dev mode, LangChain, LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, MongoDB, Firebase, Django, Flask, Express, Electron
352: Article I: Human Judgment Is Sovereign
353: Article II: Human Attention Is Primary Optimization Target
354: Article III: Knowledge Is The Product
355: Article IV: Local First By Default
358: Article VI: Replace Technologies, Preserve Architecture
361: Article X: Every Action Leaves Evidence
362: Article XI: Simplicity Is A Feature
363: Article XII: One Source Of Truth
365: Article XIV: Every Component Must Earn Its Place
366: Article XV: Architecture Exists To Reduce Decisions
